steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content: Both terms in $8x^6-40x^2$ share a numerical factor of $8$ and at least two factors of $x$. That makes $8x^2$ the greatest common factor.

steps.title: Factor each term
steps.content: Pull out $8x^2$ from each term. The first term leaves $x^4$, and the second term leaves $5$, so the remaining factor is the difference $x^4-5$: $$ 8x^6-40x^2=8x^2(x^4)-8x^2(5) $$ $$ =8x^2(x^4-5) $$ The answer is D.
